demos.name: Avro IoT Sensors — Smart Building Telemetry
demos.summary: Demonstrates Avro reading features using 5 building-floor sensor files (2,500 rows) with schema evolution (v1→v2 adds battery_pct, firmware_version) and mixed compression codecs (null, deflate). Three tables exercise multi-file reading, file_filter for single-floor extraction, max_rows for sampling, file_metadata, and Avro's self-describing schema with automatic type detection.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: CSV Advanced Options Testbench
demos.summary: Verifies that every CSV parsing option (delimiter, null_value, comment_char, skip_rows, max_rows, trim_whitespace, quote, escape) is correctly wired from the GUI to the backend. Each table exercises one option with data that exposes incorrect parsing.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Advanced SQL — Windows, CTEs & Analytics
demos.summary: Delta tables support the full power of SQL analytics including window \ functions, CTEs, and advanced aggregations, making them a drop-in \ replacement for traditional data warehouses. This...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Append-Only Ledger — Immutable Financial Compliance
demos.summary: A financial services firm builds an immutable transaction ledger using Delta's append-only property. Verifies configuration via DESCRIBE DETAIL and SHOW TBLPROPERTIES, appends regulatory batches proving count-only-grows, and contrasts against a mutable table where UPDATEs and DELETEs freely modify records.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Append-Only Event Sourcing
demos.summary: Append-only Delta tables reject UPDATE and DELETE, making them ideal for \ immutable event logs. This demo builds a 60-event order stream, reconstructs \ order lifecycles, uses window functions to measure processing times, derives \ current state from event history, appends a new batch, and proves the log \ only grows — never shrinks.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Audit Trail — Native Version-Based Compliance
demos.summary: Uses Delta's native transaction log and time travel as a tamper-proof audit trail for financial compliance. DESCRIBE HISTORY reveals every commit, VERSION AS OF reconstructs past states, and MERGE reconciles late-arriving event batches — all without manual audit columns. 42 events across 10 accounts and 4 Delta versions demonstrate immutable, version-stamped compliance.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Auto-Optimize — Automatic Compaction & Write Optimization
demos.summary: Delta Auto-Optimize uses the table properties 'delta.autoOptimize.optimizeWrite' \ and 'delta.autoOptimize.autoCompact' to automatically coalesce small Parquet files \ into larger ones during...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Basics — Create, Insert, Update, Delete
demos.summary: Delta Lake extends Parquet with a transaction log that enables full CRUD \ (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations — something plain Parquet cannot do. \ Each mutation (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Binary & Spatial Data Types
demos.summary: Delta tables store binary-like data (hashes, checksums) and spatial geometry \ as VARCHAR columns using conventions like SHA-256 hex strings and WKT \ (Well-Known Text) notation. This is essential...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Binary & Geometry Advanced
demos.summary: Advanced analytics over binary hashes, spatial WKT geometry, and cross-table document/location relationships. Covers content-hash deduplication, bounding-box spatial filters, window-function ranking, CTE joins, pairwise distance computation, LAG gap analysis, and audit-log pivoting.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Bloom Filters — Probabilistic Index for Fast Lookups
demos.summary: Bloom filters are probabilistic data structures stored alongside Delta \ data files that enable data-skipping for point lookups on high-cardinality \ columns like transaction IDs. When a query...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Change Data Feed — Track Row-Level Changes
demos.summary: Delta Change Data Feed (CDF) tracks row-level changes (inserts, updates, \ deletes) across table versions, enabling efficient incremental ETL pipelines. \ Without CDF, downstream consumers must...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Collations — Language-Aware Sorting & Comparison
demos.summary: Collation defines how strings are compared, sorted, and matched — critical for \ multilingual data where naive byte-order sorting produces incorrect results \ (e.g., placing umlauts after 'z')....
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Column Mapping — Physical & Logical Names
demos.summary: Delta Column Mapping (mode 'name') decouples logical column names in the \ schema from physical column names in Parquet files, enabling ALTER TABLE \ operations like column rename, drop, and add...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Complex Types — Structs, Arrays & Maps
demos.summary: Delta Lake supports complex data modelling through struct-like multi-column \ patterns, array-like delimited strings, and map-like key=value pairs — \ reflecting how real-world data often has...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Computed Fields — Formulas, Defaults & Derived Values
demos.summary: Computed columns are derived values calculated from other columns using \ SQL expressions, ensuring consistency without storing redundant data. \ Without a disciplined approach, formulas like...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Constraint Enforcement — NOT NULL & CHECK
demos.summary: Delta Lake CHECK constraints enforce data quality invariants at the protocol \ level, rejecting any commit whose rows violate the declared expressions. \ Without constraints, bad data silently...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Constraints Lifecycle — Validate, Mutate, Verify
demos.summary: CHECK constraints protect data quality, but the real proof is that they \ survive every DML operation. This demo creates a product catalog with three \ constraints, performs bulk UPDATEs, discontinues and deletes items, and \ re-validates after each step — proving constraints hold through the full \ mutation lifecycle.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Constraints & Table Properties
demos.summary: Delta TBLPROPERTIES store table-level configuration in the transaction log, \ including CHECK constraints and behavioral flags like delta.appendOnly. \ CHECK constraints (stored as...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Convert to Delta — Migrating from Raw Parquet
demos.summary: Converting Parquet files to Delta Lake adds a transaction log (_delta_log) \ on top of existing data files, unlocking ACID transactions, schema \ enforcement, and time travel that raw Parquet...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Cross-Timezone Scheduling — Global Conference Planner
demos.summary: Demonstrates the dual local/UTC column pattern for coordinating events across 6 global offices (New York, London, Tokyo, Sydney, Berlin, Dubai). 40 meetings with local and UTC times — queries detect room scheduling conflicts via UTC-based self-joins, find cross-office collaboration windows where 3+ offices are active, and order events chronologically regardless of timezone.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Decimal Precision & Arithmetic
demos.summary: Delta Lake stores DECIMAL types as fixed-point numbers in Parquet, preserving \ exact precision without the rounding errors inherent in DOUBLE/FLOAT types. \ This matters for financial data where...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Column Default Values
demos.summary: Column default values fill in missing fields automatically during INSERT, \ reducing boilerplate and preventing NULL values from propagating through \ analytics pipelines. When CREATE TABLE does...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Deletion Vectors — Efficient Soft Deletes
demos.summary: Deletion vectors (DVs) are a Delta Lake protocol feature that marks individual \ rows as deleted using lightweight bitmap files, avoiding the costly rewrite of \ entire Parquet data files. Without...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta DML Patterns — Complex DELETE & UPDATE
demos.summary: Delta Lake supports full DML (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) on top of immutable \ Parquet files by recording add/remove file actions in the transaction log, \ whereas traditional data lakes are...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Duration Arithmetic — Maritime Canal Transit
demos.summary: Demonstrates duration arithmetic on VARCHAR timestamps using SUBSTRING + CAST to compute transit times through a canal lock system. 35 vessel passages across 3 locks with entry/exit timestamps — queries compute transit durations in minutes, detect cross-midnight passages, and aggregate per-lock cargo throughput, all without native timestamp types.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta DV Accumulation — Performance Degradation & Recovery
demos.summary: Deletion vectors enable lightweight row-level deletes, but each DELETE creates a new DV bitmap file. In healthcare systems under GDPR/HIPAA, deletion requests arrive in small batches, causing DV files to accumulate and degrade query performance. This demo simulates three compliance deletion rounds, then recovers with OPTIMIZE.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Dynamic Partition Pruning — Smart Query Filtering
demos.summary: Dynamic partition pruning is a query optimization where the engine skips \ entire partitions of a fact table based on filter values derived from a \ joined dimension table, dramatically reducing...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Edge Cases — Empty, Wide & Minimal Tables
demos.summary: Delta tables gracefully handle boundary conditions that often trip up \ traditional data systems, including single-row tables, wide schemas, \ and empty tables. Understanding these edge cases is...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Event Sourcing Analytics — ED Patient Flow
demos.summary: A hospital emergency department tracks patient flow as an event stream: triage, admit, transfer, discharge. Uses LAG for wait times, ROW_NUMBER for current state derivation, SUM OVER for running counts, and CTEs for multi-step analytics over heterogeneous event payloads.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Feature Activation Lifecycle — Evolving Table Capabilities
demos.summary: A platform team manages a customer_events table that started plain. As requirements grow, features are activated incrementally via ALTER TABLE SET TBLPROPERTIES — first CDC for incremental ETL, then observing protocol version changes and write-time behavior at each step.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Funnel Analysis — SaaS Conversion Tracking
demos.summary: SaaS companies track users through a conversion funnel: trial → activation → subscription → renewal. This demo builds a 100-event dataset, computes stage-by-stage conversion rates using self-JOINs, then uses UPDATE to mark churned users — demonstrating Delta Lake as a mutable analytics backend.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta GDPR Data Erasure — Right to Erasure Lifecycle
demos.summary: GDPR Article 17 requires permanent deletion of personal data on request. This demo walks through a bank's compliance workflow: NULL out SSN, phone, and address for targeted customers, audit changes via DESCRIBE HISTORY, prove time travel still exposes PII in prior versions, then VACUUM to complete physical deletion.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta DESCRIBE HISTORY — Root-Cause Investigation
demos.summary: A SaaS platform's reporting dashboard shows inflated Americas revenue. A data engineer uses DESCRIBE HISTORY to list every version, then time-travels through commits to pinpoint the exact update that doubled revenue — a full diagnostic workflow without modifying the table.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta In-Commit Timestamps — Reliable Version Timing
demos.summary: In-commit timestamps embed a reliable, monotonically increasing timestamp \ directly into each Delta transaction log commit, solving the problem of \ clock skew between distributed writers....
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta JSON Event Log — Payment Gateway Analysis
demos.summary: A fintech payment gateway logs every API transaction with JSON payloads in VARCHAR columns — each transaction type (charge, refund, auth, payout) has a different payload shape. Demonstrates LIKE patterns, CASE WHEN classification, CTE analytics, and UPDATE based on JSON content.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Advanced Patterns & Conditional Logic
demos.summary: Delta MERGE INTO supports multiple WHEN MATCHED clauses with conditional \ predicates, enabling complex write patterns in a single atomic operation. \ This solves the common ETL problem of needing...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Composite Keys & Subquery Source
demos.summary: Real-world tables rarely have single-column keys. Fleet telemetry, IoT data, and multi-tenant systems use composite keys. This demo shows MERGE with a multi-column ON condition for deduplicating and upserting GPS telemetry from a delivery fleet — the standard pattern for late-arriving data corrections.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Computed Columns & CASE Logic
demos.summary: Embed business logic directly in MERGE using CASE expressions, arithmetic, and conditional logic in UPDATE SET and INSERT VALUES clauses to compute derived columns (tier, discount, priority score) at merge time.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Deduplication (Keep Latest)
demos.summary: Deduplicate a table by merging against a subquery that selects the winning row per business key using ROW_NUMBER. The self-merge pattern is idempotent and handles at-least-once delivery, pipeline retries, and overlapping batch windows.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Generating & Materializing Deletion Vectors
demos.summary: A retail product catalog sync demonstrating how MERGE generates deletion vectors (DVs) and how OPTIMIZE materializes them. An ERP system sends daily product feed updates via a three-way MERGE: matched updates create DVs for old rows, zero-stock matches trigger deletes, and new SKUs are inserted.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Idempotent Pipeline (Timestamp Guards)
demos.summary: Production pipelines must handle retries gracefully. This demo uses timestamp guards to ensure re-running the same IoT sensor batch is safe — the first run applies changes, the second run is a verified no-op.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Multi-Source Sequential Updates
demos.summary: Order fulfillment pipelines receive updates from shipping, payments, and returns systems on independent schedules. Each source MERGEs into the same order_status table, updating only its own columns while preserving data from other sources — the standard pattern for event-driven microservice data integration.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — SCD Type 2 (Slowly Changing Dimensions)
demos.summary: Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 preserves full history by expiring the current row and inserting a new version. This demo merges HR changes (promotions, transfers, salary adjustments) into an employee dimension, maintaining a complete audit trail.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Soft Delete with BY SOURCE
demos.summary: Production CDC pipelines rarely hard-delete records. This demo uses WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE with UPDATE (not DELETE) to flag stale records as inactive, with tiered rules: high-value records get flagged for review while low-value records are deactivated automatically.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — Subquery & CTE Source Patterns
demos.summary: Real-world MERGE sources require deduplication and aggregation before merging. This demo uses a CTE to aggregate raw e-commerce order events and upsert into a daily revenue summary — the pattern behind every analytics pipeline.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta MERGE — CDC Upsert with BY SOURCE
demos.summary: The MERGE INTO upsert pattern is the foundation of change data capture (CDC) pipelines, atomically applying inserts, updates, and deletes from a staging source to a target table. The WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE clause detects and removes stale target rows absent from the source feed.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: {{zone_name}}.delta_demos.quarter_dim
demos.summary: Multi-level partitioning creates a hierarchy of partition directories \(e.g., region=us-east/quarter=Q1-2024/) that enables compound pruning: \the engine can skip partitions on BOTH dimensions simultaneously. A query \filtering on region AND quarter can prune down to 1 of 16 partitions — a \93.7
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: NULL Statistics — NULL-Aware Query Optimization
demos.summary: Healthcare patient records have optional fields that start NULL and get populated over time. Delta tracks per-file NULL counts, enabling the engine to skip files with zero NULLs or all-NULL columns...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: {{zone_name}}.delta_demos.daily_orders
demos.summary: OPTIMIZE is a Delta Lake maintenance command that compacts small Parquet files \into larger, optimally-sized ones. Frequent small INSERTs — such as daily batch \loads or streaming micro-batches — create a "small files problem" that degrades \read performance due to excessive file-open overhead. \
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta OPTIMIZE — Cross-Version Data Integrity Proof
demos.summary: A logistics company tracks shipment manifests across three carriers. Daily uploads fragment the table. Weekly OPTIMIZE compacts the files, and cross-version comparison proves zero data loss: carrier breakdowns, weight sums, and row counts are identical before and after compaction.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Overflow Detection — Treasury Balance Monitoring
demos.summary: Models a multi-tier treasury ledger where account balances range from small business ($4.5M) to sovereign wealth funds ($2B). Queries detect accounts approaching the INT limit (2,147,483,647) by computing percentage proximity and risk levels. Demonstrates proactive type widening via ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN TYPE before overflow occurs, then safely processes BIGINT-range transactions for central bank reserves and pension funds.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Partition MERGE — Catalog Sync by Category
demos.summary: MERGE into a partitioned Delta table demonstrates how upsert operations target only the partition directories containing matching rows. A product catalog partitioned by category receives a supplier feed that updates Electronics, Clothing, and Home — while Sports files are never opened.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: {{zone_name}}.delta_demos.events_by_month
demos.summary: Over-partitioning is one of the most common Delta table design mistakes. When \a table is partitioned by a high-cardinality column (like customer_id), each \partition directory contains very few rows, creating hundreds or thousands of \tiny Parquet files. This "small files problem" degrades query
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Partition Replace — Monthly Sales Pipeline
demos.summary: The partition-replace pattern uses DELETE + INSERT to atomically swap an entire partition's data with a corrected dataset. A monthly sales table receives price corrections for February via partition replacement, then grows with an incremental April append — all without touching January or March.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Partition Replacement with DV Cleanup
demos.summary: A financial services firm processes monthly transaction settlements. Late-arriving invoice corrections reveal duplicates and incorrect amounts in January's partition. The partition-replace pattern deletes the entire month, inserts corrected rows, then OPTIMIZE cleans up the DVs.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Partition-Scoped Maintenance
demos.summary: In production Delta Lake deployments, OPTIMIZE can target individual partitions using WHERE clauses. This demo models a global e-commerce system partitioned by warehouse datacenter, demonstrating that mutations create partition-scoped DVs and OPTIMIZE can compact one partition without touching others.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: {{zone_name}}.delta_demos.line_metrics
demos.summary: Time-based partitioning is the most common strategy in production data lakes, \organizing files into directories by time period (year_month, event_date, etc.). \This demo partitions factory sensor readings by month and demonstrates time-range \queries with partition pruning, partition-scoped reca
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Partitioning — Organize & Query by Region
demos.summary: Delta table partitioning physically organizes data files into directories \ by partition column values, enabling partition pruning that skips entire \ file groups during queries. This dramatically...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Partitions & Deletion Vectors
demos.summary: Deletion vectors (DVs) are a Delta protocol optimization that marks rows \ as deleted in a lightweight sidecar file instead of rewriting entire \ Parquet data files, making DELETE operations...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Protocol Feature Inspection — Metadata Forensics
demos.summary: A data engineering team inherits three Delta tables and must determine which protocol features are enabled before building downstream pipelines. Uses DESCRIBE DETAIL, SHOW TABLE PROPERTIES, and DESCRIBE HISTORY to inspect protocol versions, reader/writer features, and table configuration.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta RESTORE — Advanced Recovery Scenarios
demos.summary: Delta RESTORE TO VERSION rolls an entire table back to a previous \ transaction log version, undoing all intermediate commits in a single \ atomic operation. This is a safety net for recovering...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Recovery Strategy — RESTORE vs Partition Replace
demos.summary: When only one partition is corrupted, a full RESTORE rolls back the entire table — undoing good changes in other partitions. This demo compares whole-table RESTORE with partition-scoped replacement (DELETE + INSERT FROM VERSION AS OF), demonstrating why partition replace is the surgical recovery tool for partitioned tables.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta RESTORE — Rollback to Previous Versions
demos.summary: RESTORE is a Delta Lake operation that rewinds a table to any previous \ version by writing a new commit that recreates the old snapshot. It solves \ the problem of accidental data loss: a...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Selective Recovery — VERSION AS OF Extraction
demos.summary: When only a subset of rows is corrupted but a full RESTORE would undo good changes to other rows, use VERSION AS OF to extract correct data from a historical version and surgically replace the bad rows. This demo recovers one investment portfolio while preserving rebalancing changes to two others.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta RESTORE + Time Travel — Inspect Before You Recover
demos.summary: Teaches the safe recovery workflow: use VERSION AS OF to browse every historical version, compare side by side, choose the right target, then RESTORE. Scenario: a script zeros all student grades and the registrar inspects V1 vs V2 to pick the version that preserves a legitimate curve.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta RESTORE + VACUUM — Incident Response Lifecycle
demos.summary: The complete incident response pattern for Delta tables: detect bad data via anomaly queries, RESTORE to a known-good version, VACUUM orphaned files from the bad versions, then resume operations. Demonstrates the correct order of operations and why VACUUM after RESTORE is safe.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: {{zone_name}}.delta_demos.compliance_audit
demos.summary: Row tracking is a Delta Lake protocol feature that assigns stable, unique \identifiers to each row so that changes can be traced across versions. It \enables fine-grained change detection for CDC pipelines, merge operations, \and regulatory compliance. This demo builds a financial audit trail that \
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Schema Evolution — Add Columns & NULL Filling
demos.summary: Schema evolution lets you add new columns to a Delta table without \ rewriting existing data files. This solves the real-world problem of \ evolving requirements: as your data model grows, old...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Advanced Schema Evolution — Add, Widen & Restructure
demos.summary: Advanced schema evolution demonstrates multiple rounds of ALTER TABLE ADD \ COLUMN on a single table, evolving from 5 to 8 columns across separate \ phases. This mirrors real production systems...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Flexible Ingestion — JSON to Typed Columns
demos.summary: A smart factory ingests IoT sensor telemetry with JSON metadata. Frequently-queried fields are promoted to typed columns via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN + UPDATE backfill. Demonstrates the schema promotion pattern: flexible JSON for initial ingestion, typed columns for analytical performance.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Snapshot Isolation — Portfolio Rebalancing
demos.summary: Every Delta Lake write is an atomic transaction producing a new version. This demo builds a multi-fund investment portfolio, runs OPTIMIZE to compact files, applies a price-adjustment UPDATE, then uses VERSION AS OF to prove each snapshot remains unchanged — the foundation of snapshot isolation.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Data Skipping — Range Statistics in Action
demos.summary: E-commerce order data arrives in monthly batches with naturally non-overlapping price ranges. Per-file min/max statistics allow the query engine to skip entire files when filters don't overlap a file's value range...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: String Statistics — Truncation & Bloom Filter Bridge
demos.summary: Delta stores min/max statistics for string columns, but only the first 32 characters. Short strings like SKU codes get full statistics; long strings like URLs share the same truncated prefix...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Table Properties — Configuration Lifecycle
demos.summary: Walks through the full TBLPROPERTIES lifecycle: creating a table with enableChangeDataFeed, optimizeWrite, and checkpointInterval; inspecting with SHOW TABLE PROPERTIES; modifying with ALTER TABLE SET/UNSET; and observing effects via DESCRIBE HISTORY and DESCRIBE DETAIL. A building-supply warehouse inventory with price adjustments, restocking, and discontinuation provides the real-world context.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Time Travel — Point-in-Time Joins
demos.summary: JOIN a fact table against a dimension table pinned to a historical version, ensuring each trade is valued at the exchange rate active when it executed.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Time Travel — Row-Level Change Detection
demos.summary: Detect modified, deleted, and inserted rows across Delta versions using self-JOINs and LEFT JOINs on VERSION AS OF — the fallback when Change Data Feed wasn’t enabled.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Time Travel — VACUUM Retention Boundary
demos.summary: Demonstrate that VACUUM removes old Parquet files and breaks time travel for vacuumed versions, while DESCRIBE HISTORY still shows ghost entries in the delta log.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Timestamp NTZ — Hospital Shift Handover
demos.summary: Demonstrates NTZ (No TimeZone) timestamp storage using a hospital shift handover system across 5 US hospitals in different timezones. Wall-clock shift times are stored as VARCHAR and never undergo timezone conversion — identical local times (07:00) map to different UTC instants depending on the hospital's timezone offset, and sorting by UTC reveals true chronological order while local times stay human-readable for clinical staff.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Type Widening — IoT Fleet Counter Overflow
demos.summary: Demonstrates the Delta type widening protocol feature using an IoT fleet monitoring scenario. Creates a device telemetry table with INT counters, enables type widening, promotes columns to BIGINT via ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN TYPE, then scales gateway counters and inserts high-volume edge/CDN devices with BIGINT-range values. Shows how old INT-encoded Parquet files coexist seamlessly with new BIGINT-encoded files through transparent upcasting.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Unicode Partitioning — Non-ASCII Partition Keys
demos.summary: A global CDN partitions content metadata by region names in their native script: 日本語, العربية, Русский, Français, Português. Proves that partition pruning, cross-partition queries, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE all work correctly when partition keys are non-ASCII multi-byte strings.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Unicode Roundtrip — Mutation Fidelity Proof
demos.summary: An international e-commerce marketplace stores product listings in sellers' native scripts — Japanese, Chinese, Korean, Arabic, Hebrew, Cyrillic, and Latin diacritics. Proves UTF-8 roundtrip fidelity by asserting exact Unicode values after every DML mutation: UPDATE on Unicode columns directly, DELETE with CJK LIKE predicates, and copy-on-write Parquet rewrites.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Unicode String Functions — Multi-Byte Operations
demos.summary: A multilingual customer support platform receives tickets in 15+ languages. Tests whether SQL string functions — LENGTH, UPPER, LOWER, SUBSTR, LIKE, and CONCAT — correctly handle multi-byte UTF-8 characters. LENGTH must return character count (not byte count), SUBSTR must not split multi-byte sequences, and UPPER must preserve diacritics (ü → Ü).
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Cross-Partition Updates
demos.summary: When an UPDATE predicate does not align with the partition key, Delta must create deletion vectors (DVs) in every partition that contains matching rows. This demo uses a SaaS billing platform where pricing tier changes apply globally across all regions, forcing cross-partition DV writes.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta UPDATE Expressions — Portfolio Rebalancing
demos.summary: Showcases computed expressions in UPDATE SET clauses: arithmetic adjustments (equity +5% appreciation), CASE WHEN reclassification (dynamic risk ratings based on value thresholds), and percentage-based recalculations (bond -2% decline). Three investment portfolios with 20 holdings go through a quarterly rebalancing cycle. VERSION AS OF and DESCRIBE HISTORY compare pre- and post-rebalancing snapshots.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta UPDATE Multi-Pass — ETL Pipeline Stages
demos.summary: Sequential UPDATE passes transform 30 e-commerce orders through a 3-stage ETL pipeline: Normalize (UPPER+TRIM status codes), Classify (CASE WHEN priority/shipping assignment), Enrich (total_with_tax and estimated_profit computation). VERSION AS OF inspects data at each pipeline stage, demonstrating how Delta versioning provides built-in pipeline checkpoints.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta UPDATE NULL Backfill — Patient Data Recovery
demos.summary: After a hospital database migration, many fields arrived as NULL or with sentinel values (-999, 'N/A', ''). This demo walks through 8 UPDATE passes using NULLIF, COALESCE, CASE WHEN IS NULL, and conditional SET to replace sentinels with proper NULLs and backfill missing values with defaults across 25 patient records.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta UPDATE String Cleansing — CRM Data Normalization
demos.summary: UPDATE statements with string functions clean messy data in place. Imports 25 customer records from a legacy CRM system — emails in mixed case, names with extra whitespace, unpadded account codes, and missing full names — then walks through 5 UPDATE passes using TRIM, LOWER, LPAD, concatenation (||), and UPPER to normalize every field.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta VACUUM — Cleanup Orphaned Files
demos.summary: VACUUM is a Delta Lake maintenance command that removes data files no longer \ referenced by the current table version, reclaiming storage after UPDATEs, \ DELETEs, and OPTIMIZE operations...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Maintenance Playbook — OPTIMIZE Then VACUUM
demos.summary: The standard Delta Lake maintenance sequence: OPTIMIZE compacts fragmented small files from daily micro-batch ingestion, then VACUUM RETAIN 0 HOURS purges the orphaned pre-compaction files. Uses an e-commerce order pipeline with DESCRIBE DETAIL at each stage to show physical file state while data integrity stays constant.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta VACUUM Retention Governance — Compliance-Driven Data Lifecycle
demos.summary: Compares two VACUUM retention strategies on a trade settlement table: safe retention (168 hours, preserves all recent versions for audit) versus aggressive cleanup (RETAIN 0 HOURS, destroys time travel). DESCRIBE HISTORY proves the audit trail survives both strategies — only data file availability differs. Governance-first framing for regulated environments.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta VACUUM Storage Diagnostics — Measuring Cleanup Impact
demos.summary: Walks through a product inventory lifecycle — price adjustments, discontinued removals, new arrivals — running DESCRIBE DETAIL at each stage to track version, file count, and byte size. After VACUUM, the same diagnostic queries prove cleanup worked while data remains bit-for-bit identical. The first demo to systematically use DESCRIBE DETAIL for storage auditing.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta VACUUM — Storage Cost Savings
demos.summary: Every UPDATE and DELETE in Delta Lake orphans old Parquet files via copy-on-write, silently inflating cloud storage costs. This demo uses a SaaS billing system to show how DESCRIBE DETAIL measures file accumulation and VACUUM RETAIN 0 HOURS reclaims storage — proving zero data change through before/after integrity checks.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Views & Data Masking — Role-Based Access Layers
demos.summary: Production databases serve multiple audiences with different access needs. This demo builds three role-based views on an e-commerce order table: an analyst view (no PII), a support view (masked credit cards), and an executive view (pre-aggregated regional summaries). Each view enforces its access boundary at the SQL layer.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Z-ORDER — Multi-Column Data Layout Optimization
demos.summary: Z-ORDER reorganizes data files using a space-filling curve to co-locate rows with similar values across multiple columns. This demo builds an IoT telemetry table in 3 batches, uses DESCRIBE DETAIL to observe file layout, applies OPTIMIZE ZORDER BY, then verifies compaction with DESCRIBE HISTORY.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI EDIFACT — International Trade & Logistics
demos.summary: Demonstrates UN/EDIFACT and EANCOM message parsing across 22 files covering international trade, logistics, customs clearance, and passenger data from 7 directory versions (D:96A through D:13B) and 6 syntax identifiers. 17 distinct message types (ORDERS, INVOIC, IFCSUM, CUSCAR, PAXLST, etc.) with materialized field extraction, multi-message files, and full JSON transaction access.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI HIPAA Claim Status — Inquiry & Authorization Tracking
demos.summary: Demonstrates HIPAA claim status tracking and prior authorization review using CASE on composite status codes, LIKE pattern matching, and multi-table JOINs. Three transactions cover the 276/277 claim status request/response cycle and 278 health services review. Queries classify composite STC codes (P3:317 = pending, F0:3 = finalized) using LIKE, correlate request amounts with response statuses, and JOIN two specialized tables for unified lifecycle views.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI HIPAA Claims Financial — Charge & Remittance Analysis
demos.summary: Demonstrates financial analysis of HIPAA X12 healthcare claims using SUM, CAST, HAVING, and JOIN — SQL features not covered by any other EDI demo. Four transactions cover 837 professional/dental/institutional claims and 835 remittance advice. Queries compute total charges with SUM, filter with HAVING, calculate write-off percentages with CAST and arithmetic, and JOIN claim charges with remittance payments for end-to-end reconciliation.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI HIPAA Eligibility & Enrollment — Coverage Verification
demos.summary: Demonstrates HIPAA eligibility verification and benefit enrollment analysis using CTEs, JOINs, and COALESCE — SQL patterns unique across all EDI demos. Three transactions cover the 270/271 eligibility request/response cycle and 834 benefit enrollment. A CTE pairs request with response by matching TRN trace numbers, and a JOIN correlates eligibility context with enrollment plan data.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI HIPAA Healthcare — Claims Lifecycle
demos.summary: Demonstrates X12 EDI transaction parsing for the healthcare claims lifecycle. 11 transactions spanning 9 HIPAA 5010 transaction types: eligibility verification (270/271), claim status (276/277), healthcare claims (837 Professional/Dental/Institutional), payment remittance (835), benefit enrollment (834), prior authorization (278), and payment order (820). Covers materialized path extraction and full JSON access.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI X12 Supply Chain — Order-to-Cash Lifecycle
demos.summary: Demonstrates X12 EDI transactions spanning the full supply chain order-to-cash lifecycle. 14 real-world transactions across 8 types: purchase orders (850), invoices (810), PO acknowledgments (855), ship notices (856), shipment & billing (857), receiving advice (861), functional acknowledgment (997), and application advice (824). Multi-version X12 (00204, 00401) with trading partner diversity.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI TRADACOMS — UK Retail Orders & Utilities
demos.summary: Demonstrates TRADACOMS — the UK-specific EDI standard used in retail and utilities. 4 files containing 15 total messages cover purchase orders, product planning forecasts, and utility bills. Features multi-message file parsing, STX envelope extraction, TRADACOMS escape character decoding (?' and ?+), and EAN-13 product codes.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI X12 Transportation — Freight Lifecycle
demos.summary: Demonstrates X12 EDI transactions spanning the full freight lifecycle. 12 files across 8 types: load tendering (204), freight invoicing (210), shipment tracking (214), rail transport (404), warehouse operations (945), payment remittance (820), price catalogs (832), and load tender response (990). Multi-version X12 (00204 through 005010) with multi-modal transportation.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Excel Sales Analytics — Superstore Orders
demos.summary: Demonstrates all Excel reading features using 4 years of Superstore sales data (2014–2017, 16,676 orders). Five tables exercise sheet_name, has_header, skip_rows, max_rows, range, trim_whitespace, null_values, empty_cell_handling, infer_schema_rows, file_filter, multi-file reading, file_metadata, and automatic type inference for dates, numbers, and strings.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR Clinical Observations — Vital Signs & Lab Results
demos.summary: Demonstrates FHIR R5 Observation resource ingestion across vital signs (body weight, height, BMI, blood pressure, temperature, SpO2), lab results (glucose, CO2, hemoglobin), and clinical assessments (Glasgow Coma Scale). 100 bulk heart rate readings plus 14 individual clinical observations. Covers deep nested JSON flattening, schema evolution across observation types, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR Clinical Records — Conditions, Procedures & Allergies
demos.summary: Demonstrates advanced FHIR R5 resource ingestion across three clinical resource types: 8 Condition resources (diagnoses with SNOMED CT coding, severity, body site), 8 Procedure resources (surgeries with performers and outcomes), and 6 AllergyIntolerance resources (allergies with reaction history and criticality). Three tables showcase multi-resource-type directory scanning, deep nested arrays, and cross-resource references.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR Medications — Prescriptions & Coverage
demos.summary: Demonstrates FHIR R5 MedicationRequest and Coverage resource ingestion. 12 prescriptions with contained Medication resources, dosage instructions, dispense requests, and substitution rules. 4 Coverage resources with insurance classes and beneficiary references. Covers json_paths for preserving complex subtrees, contained resources, and deep FHIR nesting.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR Patient Demographics — Basics
demos.summary: Demonstrates FHIR R5 Patient resource ingestion from both bulk NDJSON exports (50 patients) and individual JSON files (7 detailed patients). Two tables show flat bulk demographics vs rich nested FHIR data with names, telecom, addresses, and managing organizations. Covers NDJSON format, column_mappings, schema evolution, and file_metadata.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR XML — Clinical Patients & Observations
demos.summary: Demonstrates HL7 FHIR R4 clinical data in native XML format. FHIR XML stores all primitive values as @value attributes. 8 Patient and 8 Observation XML files showcase FHIR namespace handling, @value extraction, exclude_paths for narrative XHTML, xml_paths for component/referenceRange subtrees, repeating element join_comma, deep XPath column_mappings, and schema evolution.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Cypher Queries — Pattern Matching & Algorithms
demos.summary: Cypher query language demo on a 5-person social graph. Pattern matching (MATCH, WHERE, RETURN), variable-length paths, and 15+ graph algorithm procedures: PageRank, betweenness, closeness, degree centrality, connected components, Louvain community detection, triangle counting, shortest path, BFS/DFS, MST, and KNN similarity.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Dolphins Social Network — Marine Biology Graph
demos.summary: Social associations between 62 bottlenose dolphins in Doubtful Sound, New Zealand (Lusseau et al., 2003). 159 undirected edges. Known community structure, triangle count (285), and max degree (12).
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Email-Eu-core — European Research Institution
demos.summary: Directed email communication network from a European research institution (SNAP). 1,005 persons, 25,571 directed edges. 42 ground-truth department communities. 10 weakly connected components, 105,461 triangles, diameter 7.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Flattened Mode — Direct Column Access
demos.summary: Demonstrates the flattened property storage mode for graph tables. All vertex and edge properties stored as individual columns — fastest mode with full predicate pushdown. 5-person social graph with 6 directed friendships. Queries cover traversal, aggregation, 2-hop neighbors, reciprocal edges, and department connectivity.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Hybrid Mode — Columns + JSON Extras
demos.summary: Demonstrates the hybrid property storage mode for graph tables. Frequently queried properties as columns for fast access, optional/extensible properties in a JSON extras column. Same 5-person social graph showing mixed column + JSON access patterns and the balanced trade-off between performance and flexibility.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph JSON Mode — Schema-Free Properties
demos.summary: Demonstrates the JSON property storage mode for graph tables. All vertex and edge properties in a single JSON string column — most flexible mode for schema-free evolution. Same 5-person social graph with JSON extraction functions (json_get_str, json_get_int, json_get_float), dynamic property discovery, and array access.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Zachary's Karate Club — Classic Graph Benchmark
demos.summary: The most studied graph in network science (Zachary, 1977). 34 club members, 78 undirected edges. Ground-truth 2-faction split for community detection verification. Known centrality values, degree distribution, and connected component structure.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: LDBC Social Network Benchmark — Full Model Graph Verification
demos.summary: Full LDBC Social Network Benchmark (SF0.1) with 8 entity types, 23 relationship types, and ~1.2M rows. Verifies graph algorithms (PageRank, shortest path, Louvain, centrality, BFS) and Cypher/SQL queries against official LDBC golden values. Demonstrates mixed SQL + Cypher queries joining graph algorithm results with Delta tables.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: NetScience — Coauthorship Network
demos.summary: Coauthorship network of 1,461 network-science researchers with 2,742 weighted edges (Newman, 2006). Multiple disconnected components, non-uniform weights, and modularity ~0.95 reflecting clear research-group structure.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Political Books — Partisan Co-Purchasing Network
demos.summary: 105 US political books with 441 co-purchasing edges from Amazon (Krebs/Newman). Ground-truth 3-community partition (liberal/neutral/conservative). Max degree 25, avg clustering ~0.488.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Social Network — 100-Node Company Graph
demos.summary: Large-scale graph analytics on a 100-employee company social network with 300+ directed connections across 8 departments and 5 cities. Deterministic generate_series() data generation. Queries cover degree centrality, department cross-pollination, influence scoring, 2-hop neighborhoods, team clustering, bridge detection, and mentor network analysis.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Stress Test — 1M-Node Performance Benchmark
demos.summary: Extreme-scale graph performance benchmark: 1,000,000 vertex nodes and 5,000,000+ directed edges across 20 departments, 15 cities, and 200 project teams. Stress-tests graph rendering, layout algorithms, Cypher engine algorithms (PageRank, Louvain, BFS, shortest path, MST), and memory handling at massive scale.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: H3 GPS Fleet Tracker
demos.summary: Demonstrates H3 hexagonal spatial indexing with Delta tables for GPS fleet tracking. Creates 10,000 GPS pings across 5 world cities, defines region boundaries as WKT polygons, and performs O(1) spatial joins via H3 cell matching. Verifies 21 H3 functions with known values: coordinate conversion, grid topology (ring/disk counts), cell hierarchy, area/edge metrics, polyfill, and spatial join accuracy.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: H3 Point-in-Polygon — 1M Row Performance
demos.summary: Demonstrates blazing-fast point-in-polygon queries using H3 hexagonal spatial indexing. 1,000,000 ride-share driver GPS positions are checked against 12 pricing zones across 8 world cities using O(1) H3 cell matching instead of O(n×m) polygon intersection. Progressive queries build from 'what is H3' to million-row analytics with 10 automated PASS/FAIL checks.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: HL7 Clinical Workflows — Documents, Scheduling & Edge Cases
demos.summary: Demonstrates HL7 v2 clinical workflow messages beyond core ADT and lab types. MDM^T02 document management with full History & Physical narrative, SIU^S12 appointment scheduling from two systems, and comprehensive edge-case testing with escape sequences (\F\, \T\, \S\, \R\, \E\, \X0D\), empty fields, and multi-line text.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: HL7 Lab Orders & Results — ORM/ORU Workflow
demos.summary: Models the complete laboratory workflow using HL7 v2 ORM (Order) and ORU (Observation Result) messages. 3 ORM orders and 5 ORU results from 4+ LIS/EHR systems across HL7 versions 2.3–2.5.1. Covers materialized PID/OBR/OBX extraction, value type handling (NM, ST, TX, SN), abnormal flag detection, escape sequence decoding, and cross-system unification.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: HL7 Patient Administration — ADT Lifecycle
demos.summary: Demonstrates HL7 v2 ADT (Admit-Discharge-Transfer) messages from 6 EHR systems across 5 HL7 versions (2.3–2.6). 8 real-world messages cover admission (A01), demographics update (A08), and discharge (A03). Features MSH header extraction, materialized PID/PV1/EVN paths, escape sequence decoding, Z-segment preservation, and cross-EHR field mapping.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: JSON Country Factbook — Deep Nesting & Schema Evolution
demos.summary: Demonstrates deep JSON nesting (3+ levels), schema evolution with NULL filling, and selective path extraction using CIA World Factbook data for 10 African countries. One-object-per-file format with optional Terrorism/Space sections. Two tables: countries (flattened overview with preserve_original) and country_economy (GDP, sectors, trade). Covers exclude_paths, column_mappings, max_depth, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: JSON Customers — Basics
demos.summary: Demonstrates JSON fundamentals: creating an external table from a JSON array file, applying column_mappings for clean names ($.first → first_name, $.last → last_name), and using type inference for proper timestamp handling. 200 CRM customer records with include_paths, max_depth, and file_metadata.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: JSON Music Catalog — Nested Arrays & Explode
demos.summary: Demonstrates nested JSON flattening with array explosion. 347 music albums with nested vendor objects and track detail arrays across 3 genre-based catalog files. Two tables: album_tracks (exploded, 3,503 rows) and album_summary (347 rows with vendor as JSON blob). Covers explode_paths, json_paths, column_mappings, default_array_handling, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: JSON Subtree Capture — json_paths
demos.summary: Demonstrates json_paths for capturing complex JSON subtrees as serialized string columns instead of flattening. Two tables show the same property listing data: one with location and pricing subtrees captured as JSON blobs (json_paths), one fully flattened for comparison. Covers json_paths, column_mappings, include_paths, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Northwind Trading Company
demos.summary: Classic relational database with customers, orders, products, employees, and suppliers. 13 interconnected tables demonstrating joins, aggregations, and business analytics.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: ORC Server Logs — HTTP Access Analytics
demos.summary: Demonstrates ORC reading features using 5 server access log files (2,500 rows) with schema evolution (v1→v2 adds request_body_bytes, cache_hit). Three tables exercise multi-file reading, file_filter for single-server extraction, max_rows for sampling, file_metadata, and ORC's self-describing schema with automatic type detection from file footers.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Parquet Supply Chain — Order Analytics
demos.summary: Demonstrates Parquet reading features using 14 quarterly order files (2012–2016, 73,089 rows) organized in year-based subdirectories. Four tables exercise recursive directory scanning, file_filter for year/quarter extraction, max_rows for sampling, row_group_filter for predicate pushdown, file_metadata for traceability, and Parquet's self-describing schema.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Protobuf Address Book — Contact Directory
demos.summary: Demonstrates Protocol Buffers (proto3) binary file reading using the classic Google addressbook schema. 3 team roster files (13 contacts, 22 phone numbers) read into flattened contacts and exploded phone-number tables. Covers nested message flattening, repeated field handling (join vs. explode), enum decoding (PhoneType), well-known types (Timestamp to ISO 8601), sparse data, and column mappings.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Sales Data Quickstart
demos.summary: Minimal sales dataset to get started quickly. Two CSV files showing basic queries, filtering, and aggregations.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: XML Books — Schema Evolution
demos.summary: Demonstrates XML schema evolution across 5 catalog export files spanning 2000–2004. Each year adds new elements (isbn, publisher, rating, edition, series) and one drops description. Verifies the union schema, NULL filling, and attribute extraction.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: XML E-Commerce — Order Line Explosion
demos.summary: Demonstrates deep XML nesting (3+ levels), explode_paths for one-row-per-line-item, CDATA sections, exclude_paths, column_mappings, and self-closing flag elements on e-commerce order batch data with 5 orders and 11 line items.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: XML NYT News — RSS Feed Analysis
demos.summary: Demonstrates XML namespace handling (4 namespaces: dc, media, atom, nyt), repeating element modes (JoinComma and Explode), self-closing element attributes, column_mappings, exclude_paths, and multi-file reading across 7 NYT RSS feed exports with 231 news articles and 2023 category keywords.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: XML Subtree Capture — xml_paths & nested_output_format
demos.summary: Demonstrates xml_paths for capturing complex XML subtrees as serialized string columns instead of flattening. Two tables show the same product catalog with subtrees (specifications, supplier) captured as JSON objects or raw XML fragments via nested_output_format. Top-level fields are flattened normally alongside captured subtrees.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Z-ORDER vs Partitioning — Choosing the Right Data Layout
demos.summary: The most common Delta table design question: should I partition or Z-ORDER? This demo builds the same e-commerce dataset in two tables — one partitioned by region, one Z-ORDERed — and runs side-by-side queries showing where each strategy wins.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

categories.name: Delta Fundamentals
categories.description: Core Delta operations: CRUD, schema evolution, MERGE upsert, partitioning, computed columns, constraints, complex types, and decimal precision

categories.name: Delta DML & MERGE
categories.description: Advanced data manipulation: complex DELETE/UPDATE patterns, multi-clause MERGE, generated columns, constraint enforcement, and analytics SQL

categories.name: Delta Schema & Data Types
categories.description: Schema evolution, column mapping, type widening, binary/spatial data, variant types, timestamps, collations, and Parquet-to-Delta conversion

categories.name: Delta Versioning & History
categories.description: Time travel, RESTORE rollback, Change Data Feed, VACUUM cleanup, row tracking, and in-commit timestamps for version management

categories.name: Delta Performance & Layout
